questions.questionText: The Mental Healthcare Act 2017 (India) provides for:
questions.optionB: Right to access mental healthcare, advance directives, nominated representatives, prohibition of chaining, decriminalization of suicide attempt, mental health review boards
questions.explanation: MHCA 2017 (India): landmark legislation. Key provisions: (1) Right to access mental healthcare and treatment. (2) Advance directives (person can specify preferred treatment while competent). (3) Nominated representative. (4) Prohibition of chaining of mentally ill persons. (5) Decriminalization of attempted suicide (Section 115 — presumption of severe stress). (6) Mental Health Review Boards (MHRB) for oversight. (7) Insurance parity (mental illness covered like physical illness). (8) Restriction on ECT (only with muscle relaxant and anesthesia, prohibited in minors). Replaced Mental Health Act 1987. Reference: Niraj Ahuja — MHCA 2017.
